outbound_official.notes: Source is the official Azerbaijan Ministry of Foreign Affairs page listing visa-free countries (mfa.gov.az/en/category/visa/visa-free-countries). The page organises outbound access into groups: Group II lists bilateral agreements where ALL passport types (including ordinary/civil) are covered; Group III lists countries where Azerbaijan citizens (holding civil/ordinary passports) may travel unilaterally without a visa for the stated period. The diplomatic/service-passport-only bilateral list (Group I, 79 countries per the April 2026 PDF) is excluded here as it does not apply to ordinary passport holders. North Macedonia (Group III, March 2022–March 2023) has expired. Hong Kong SAR under Group III is listed for diplomatic/service only. The page URL asof date reflects the latest PDF on the page (29 April 2026).

visa_policy.notes: Azerbaijan's official MFA page lists visa-free countries in four groups. Group I: diplomatic/service passport bilateral (PDF list). Group II: all passport types bilateral - 17 countries including CIS states, Turkey, UAE, Serbia, Albania, Morocco, China, Maldives, Qatar. Group III: unilateral visa-free for Azerbaijani citizens traveling OUTBOUND (e.g., to Iran for 15 days, Malaysia 30 days, Montenegro 90 days, Bosnia 90 days) - these are NOT inbound visa-free grants to Azerbaijan. Group IV: unilateral visa-free for foreign ordinary passport holders entering Azerbaijan - Hong Kong/Macao SAR (Feb 2 2026–Feb 2 2027, 3 entries, 30 days) and Saudi Arabia, Oman, Bahrain, Kuwait (Feb 15 2026–Feb 15 2027, 3 entries, 30 days; 4th entry requires a visa). The e-visa (ASAN Visa) at evisa.gov.az issues single-entry electronic visas valid for 30 days stay; standard processing 3 working days, urgent 3 hours; fee $20 USD (US State Dept) plus a separate service fee (~$3-5 USD). The airport visa on arrival (VOA) is confirmed by migration.gov.az/en/page/49 for 14 nationalities: Qatar, Oman, Saudi Arabia, Bahrain, Kuwait, Japan, Indonesia, Iran, China, South Korea, Malaysia, Israel, UAE, Singapore. Entry and transit is refused to citizens of Kosovo. Citizens of Armenia may face detention or denial of entry. Entry by land border is restricted; foreign nationals must use air entry. Foreigners staying more than 15 days must register with the State Migration Service within 15 days of arrival.

credential.subtype: resident visa of the United Arab Emirates

conditional_access.conditions: Holders of a resident visa (residence permit) of the United Arab Emirates can be issued a visa at the international airports of the Republic of Azerbaijan. UAE resident visa must have at least 6 months validity remaining. Traveller must hold a passport (not a travel document) valid for at least 4 months. Excludes nationals of Syria, Iraq, and Afghanistan.

Citizens of the relevant foreign country holding diplomatic or service (or official/special) passport are visa-free under bilateral intergovernmental agreements (Group I). 79 countries listed in the official PDF dated 29 April 2026. Permitted stay varies by country (30 or 90 days). Libya temporarily suspended. See PDF for full per-country details.

US citizens traveling on the New York-Baku flight may obtain a 30-day visa at Heydar Aliyev International Airport (Baku). This is a flight-route-specific airport visa provision; not applicable to US citizens arriving on other routes (who must use e-visa).

admission_restrictions.notes: Entry and transit refused to citizens of Kosovo, even if not leaving the aircraft and proceeding by the same flight. Azerbaijan does not recognize Kosovo's independence.

Law enforcement officials have at times detained individuals from Armenia or with Armenian surnames for questioning or denied them entry. Individuals bearing 'Republic of Artsakh' entry stamps risk questioning or refusal of entry. Policy is confirmed by US State Department (travel.state.gov). This is not a statutory blanket ban but results in frequent denial in practice.

cbi.notes: No citizenship-by-investment program found on official government sources. The State Migration Service (migration.gov.az) offers investment-based residence permits but no direct citizenship-by-investment pathway. Citizenship grants are handled by presidential decree and no investment-for-citizenship scheme is advertised on any official government site.

programs.name: Investment-Based Temporary Residence Permit
programs.notes: Foreigners can obtain a temporary residence permit by: (1) possessing real estate worth at least AZN 100,000 in Azerbaijan; (2) holding a fixed-term bank deposit of at least AZN 100,000 in Azerbaijani banks; (3) owning government securities or state-majority (51%+) company investment securities with nominal value of at least AZN 100,000; or (4) making a general economic investment of at least AZN 500,000. Initial permit issued for period requested (up to 1 year); extendable each time for up to 2 years.

programs.name: Investment-Based Permanent Residence Permit
programs.notes: Foreigners can obtain a permanent residence permit after 2 years of continuous temporary residence by: (1) possessing real estate worth at least AZN 200,000; (2) holding a fixed-term bank deposit of at least AZN 200,000; (3) owning securities with nominal value of at least AZN 200,000; or (4) maintaining general economic investment of at least AZN 500,000. Permanent permits issued for 5 years, extendable for further 5-year periods.

programs.name: Highly Qualified Specialist Temporary Residence Permit
programs.notes: Highly qualified specialists in spheres of economy, industry, military, science, culture, sports, etc. may obtain a temporary residence permit for up to 1 year (extendable up to 2 years). Requires a justified application from the inviting authority, enterprise, or organization. After 2 years of continuous temporary residence, transition to permanent residence is possible.

programs.name: ASAN Visa - Urgent Electronic Visa
programs.eligibility: Nationals of countries eligible for Azerbaijan e-visa (as listed at evisa.gov.az/countries)
programs.processing_time: 3 hours (regardless of holidays and non-working days)
programs.notes: The ASAN Visa urgent service processes single-entry e-visa applications within 3 hours for any eligible nationality. Standard processing takes 3 working days. The visa is valid for 30 days stay in Azerbaijan.

visa_types.name: ASAN e-Visa (Tourism)
visa_types.purpose: Allows foreign nationals to enter Azerbaijan for tourism and leisure purposes. Issued electronically via the ASAN Visa system.
visa_types.notes: State fee $20 USD (confirmed by US State Dept travel.state.gov as of June 2026; azerbaican.az lists $20 + $3 service fee). Standard processing: 3 working days; urgent processing: 3 hours (available 24/7 including holidays). Available to nationals of approximately 90+ countries as listed at evisa.gov.az/countries. Single entry, 30 days stay. Registration required within 15 days for stays exceeding 15 days.

visa_types.name: ASAN e-Visa (Business)
visa_types.purpose: Allows foreign nationals to enter Azerbaijan for business purposes including meetings, conferences, and commercial activities. Issued electronically via the ASAN Visa system.
visa_types.notes: Standard processing: 3 working days; urgent processing: 3 hours. Fee $20 USD. Same ASAN Visa system as tourist e-visa; purpose is selected during application. Available to nationals eligible for Azerbaijan e-visa.

visa_types.name: Transit Visa
visa_types.purpose: Issued to foreigners and stateless persons to allow transit passage through the territory of Azerbaijan to a third country. Available as single or double transit visa.
visa_types.notes: Validity: 30 days from date of issue; maximum stay: 5 days. Issued based on transport route (air, water, rail, road, etc.). Both single and two-entry (double) transit visas are available. Applied for at Azerbaijani diplomatic missions abroad.

visa_types.name: Labor (Work) Entry Visa
visa_types.purpose: Allows foreign nationals to enter Azerbaijan for the purpose of taking up employment. Must be combined with a work permit issued by the State Migration Service.
visa_types.notes: Entry visa is issued for the 'labor' purpose. A separate work permit must be obtained from the State Migration Service. Work permit is valid for 1 year (or the duration of the employment contract if shorter); extendable for up to 1 year per extension (up to 2 years in liberated territories). Employer must demonstrate no available qualified Azerbaijani citizens. Some categories are exempt from the work permit requirement (diplomatic staff, international organization staff, highly skilled migrants, athletes, etc.).

visa_types.name: Science and Education Entry Visa
visa_types.purpose: Allows foreign nationals to enter Azerbaijan for educational or scientific purposes, including enrollment at Azerbaijani educational institutions.
visa_types.notes: Issued for the 'science and education' purpose category under ordinary entry visas. Students enrolled full-time at Azerbaijani institutions are also eligible for a Temporary Residence Permit. Multi-entry variants valid up to 2 years may be available for long-term study.

visa_types.name: Medical Treatment Entry Visa
visa_types.purpose: Allows foreign nationals to enter Azerbaijan for the purpose of receiving medical treatment at Azerbaijani medical facilities.
visa_types.notes: Issued under the 'treatment' purpose category of ordinary entry visas. Applicants must provide documentation from an Azerbaijani medical institution confirming the treatment.

visa_types.name: Personal/Family Entry Visa
visa_types.purpose: Allows foreign nationals to enter Azerbaijan for personal and family visits, including visiting close relatives who are Azerbaijani citizens or residents.
visa_types.notes: Issued under the 'personal travel' purpose category of ordinary entry visas. Family members of Azerbaijani citizens or temporary/permanent residents may also qualify for Temporary Residence Permit. Close family relationship with an Azerbaijani citizen is one of the grounds for TRP.

visa_types.name: Humanitarian/Culture/Sport Entry Visa
visa_types.purpose: Allows foreign nationals to enter Azerbaijan for humanitarian, cultural, or sporting activities and events.
visa_types.notes: Issued under 'humanitarian' or 'culture and sport' purpose categories of ordinary entry visas. Athletes and coaches performing in Azerbaijan may be exempt from work permit requirements.

visa_types.name: Diplomatic / Service Entry Visa
visa_types.purpose: Issued to foreign officials holding diplomatic or service (official/special) passports traveling to Azerbaijan for official purposes. Citizens of 79 countries with bilateral agreements may enter visa-free on these passport types.
visa_types.notes: Diplomatic and service visas form a separate category from ordinary visas. Holders of diplomatic/service passports from 79 countries (Group I per MFA PDF, April 29 2026) enter visa-free for 30 or 90 days depending on the bilateral agreement. Libya temporarily suspended. For non-exempt countries, diplomatic/service visa must be obtained in advance from Azerbaijani diplomatic missions.

visa_types.name: Investment-Based Temporary Residence Permit
visa_types.purpose: Grants temporary residence in Azerbaijan to foreign nationals who make qualifying investments in real estate, bank deposits, government securities, or the general economy.
visa_types.notes: Minimum investment thresholds (in AZN): real estate >= AZN 100,000; fixed-term bank deposit >= AZN 100,000; government or state-majority-company securities >= AZN 100,000 nominal value; or general economic investment >= AZN 500,000. Initial permit issued for up to 1 year (3 years for highly skilled migrants); extendable by up to 2 years each time. After 2 years of temporary residence, eligible to apply for permanent residence permit.

visa_types.name: Highly Qualified Specialist Temporary Residence Permit
visa_types.purpose: Grants temporary residence in Azerbaijan to foreign nationals who are recognized as highly qualified specialists in fields of economy, industry, military, science, culture, or sports.
visa_types.notes: Initial permit for up to 3 years; extendable for up to 3 years per extension. Requires a justified application from the inviting Azerbaijani authority, enterprise, or organization. Exempt from work permit requirement. After 2 years of continuous temporary residence, eligible to apply for permanent residence.

visa_types.name: Visa on Arrival - Airport Visa
visa_types.purpose: Allows nationals of eligible countries to obtain a 30-day single-entry visa upon arrival at international airports of Azerbaijan without pre-applying.
visa_types.notes: Available at international airports for nationals of: Qatar, Oman, Saudi Arabia, Bahrain, Kuwait, Japan (fee reportedly waived), Indonesia, Iran, China, South Korea, Malaysia, Israel, UAE, Singapore. UAE nationals with residence permits can use the conditional UAE-resident-visa VOA channel. Japanese fee reportedly waived. Several of these nationalities (Saudi Arabia, Oman, Bahrain, Kuwait) are also currently visa-free under Group IV (Feb 15 2026–Feb 15 2027). US citizens on New York-Baku flights can also obtain an airport visa.

visa_types.name: Visa on Arrival - UAE Resident Visa Holders
visa_types.purpose: Allows holders of a valid UAE residence permit to obtain a visa at international airports of Azerbaijan without pre-applying.
visa_types.notes: Conditional on holding a UAE residence permit (resident visa) with at least 6 months remaining validity. Traveler must hold a valid passport (not a travel document) valid for at least 4 months. Excludes nationals of Syria, Iraq, and Afghanistan. Issued at international airports only.

validation_notes: CORRECTED: Iran e-visa entry previously stated 'Also eligible for unilateral visa-free (Group III, 15 days).' This was factually wrong. Group III on the MFA page lists countries where AZERBAIJANI CITIZENS can travel visa-free outbound (e.g., Azerbaijanis going to Iran get 15 days). It does NOT grant Iranians visa-free entry to Azerbaijan. The note has been corrected to clarify this directional error.

CORRECTED: Bahrain, Kuwait, Oman, Saudi Arabia e-visa notes updated to explicitly state '4th entry requires a valid visa' per the evisa.gov.az footnote (previously said 'visa-free entry (Feb 15 2026–Feb 15 2027, up to 3 entries)' without the 4th-entry requirement).

VERIFIED: Group II countries (Belarus, Georgia, Kazakhstan, Kyrgyzstan, Moldova, Uzbekistan, Russia, Tajikistan, Ukraine, Turkey, Qatar, UAE, Serbia, Albania, Morocco, China, Maldives) all confirmed against live MFA page - all nationalities and stay durations match.

VERIFIED: Group IV temporary visa-free entries (Hong Kong/Macao Feb 2 2026–Feb 2 2027; Saudi Arabia/Oman/Bahrain/Kuwait Feb 15 2026–Feb 15 2027) confirmed still active (today is June 29 2026, within the valid window).

VERIFIED: RBI investment thresholds confirmed from migration.gov.az/en/page/73 - AZN 100,000 for TRP (real estate/deposits/securities), AZN 500,000 (general economy); AZN 200,000 for PRP (real estate/deposits/securities), AZN 500,000 (general economy). All match existing record.

NOTED: North Macedonia appeared in Group III (outbound for Azerbaijanis) but the MFA page lists it with dates 'March 16, 2022 to March 15, 2023' - this has expired. North Macedonia is correctly in e_visa for inbound travelers.

VALIDATION 2026-06-29: mfa.gov.az and all its subdomains returned SSL certificate errors during this run - official MFA page content could not be re-fetched directly. Group II and Group IV entries preserved as previously confirmed.

VALIDATION 2026-06-29: evisa.gov.az/countries returned HTTP 403 in this run. E-visa country list could not be re-confirmed against the official page. Existing country list is preserved. Search results indicate ~95 countries eligible as of 2026 (vs 79 previously noted - actual count in record is higher; the '79' was a miscounted estimate). The azerbaijan.az official portal references '81 countries' for e-visa.

VALIDATION 2026-06-29: migration.gov.az/en/page/49 loaded successfully and confirmed airport visa on arrival for 14 nationalities: Qatar, Oman, Saudi Arabia, Bahrain, Kuwait, Japan, Indonesia, Iran, China, South Korea, Malaysia, Israel, UAE, Singapore. ADDED: visa_on_arrival was previously empty ([]) - this was a significant gap. All 14 nationalities now added to visa_on_arrival section.
VALIDATION 2026-06-29: migration.gov.az/en/page/73 loaded successfully. RBI investment thresholds re-confirmed. All match existing record.

VALIDATION 2026-06-29: azerbaijan.az/en/related-information/215 loaded. Confirmed visa on arrival for Malaysia, Oman, Japan, China, Qatar, South Korea, Saudi Arabia, Singapore, Turkey, Kuwait, UAE. Also confirms e-visa fee $20 USD + $3 service fee and 81-country e-visa list.

ADDED 2026-06-29: admission_restrictions section added. Kosovo citizens refused entry and transit (confirmed via Wikipedia/multiple sources; no official AZ government page directly states this but it is a known and consistent policy). Armenia entry restrictions confirmed by US State Department (travel.state.gov).
ADDED 2026-06-29: US citizen route-specific airport VOA (New York-Baku flight) promoted from gaps to conditional_access based on migration.gov.az/en/page/49 confirmation.

NOTED 2026-06-29: E-visa fee: US State Dept confirms $20 USD; azerbaijan.az lists $20 + $3 service fee; Wikipedia cites $25 + $5. The official azerbaijan.az government portal is the most authoritative source available and lists $20 state fee + $3 service fee. Record updated in notes to reflect both components.
gaps: Group I visa-free list (diplomatic/service passport holders) is published as a PDF (https://mfa.gov.az/files/VIZA/Visa%20Free%20Countries%20List%20-%2029%20April%202026.pdf); 79 countries are listed; individual per-country stay durations vary (30 or 90 days) and should be consulted in the PDF.
Airport visa on arrival fee for most nationalities not specified in migration.gov.az/en/page/49 text. Multiple third-party sources cite $20-30 USD but no official fee schedule was fetched directly.
Kosovo entry refusal: no official Azerbaijan government web page was found directly stating this policy. It is reflected in Wikipedia and travel advisories but not an official .gov.az source.

Armenia entry restriction: US State Dept confirms officials have at times denied entry; no statutory blanket ban was found from AZ official sources. Classified as 'high_risk_denied_entry' rather than 'refused_entry'.

E-visa eligible country count: evisa.gov.az/countries returns 403; azerbaijan.az references 81 countries; search results indicate ~95 countries as of 2026. Exact current count and any newly added countries cannot be confirmed without successful fetch of evisa.gov.az/countries.
mfa.gov.az SSL certificate errors prevented re-fetching of the main visa-free country page in the June 2026 validation run. Group II and Group IV data preserved from prior confirmed session.
Dual citizenship policy for Azerbaijan not confirmed from official sources in this research session.
